title: Peltopsis perfoliata
taxonomy: Peltopsis perfoliata is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae. It is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is part of the order Celastrales and class Magnoliopsida, which indicates its classification as an angiosperm. Furthermore, its classification can be broken down to the kingdom Plantae, indicating its status as a plant. Its classification is a part of the larger taxonomy of flowering plants.

parts: Peltopsis perfoliata, also known as the desert marigold, is a plant with a rich history of traditional use. The medicinal parts of Peltopsis perfoliata are its flowers, leaves, and roots. intro: Peltopsis perfoliata, also known as Rattlesnake Weed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Peltopsis perfoliata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions.
